Wednesday, Jul 8 · 4:45 PM to 8:00 PM CEST

LEAP HQ

Østergade 36, 1.sal, 1100 København K · Copenhagen

Copenhagen dbt Meetup vol. 10

Hosted by Anders Boje H. and 2 others

Copenhagen dbt Meetup
4.7
74 reviews
Details

Tenth dbt Meetup in Copenhagen
dbt Meetups gather the analytics engineering community to share knowledge, exchange ideas, and learn from real-world experiences. Join fellow data practitioners in Copenhagen to connect, discuss best practices, and explore the latest developments in dbt and the modern data stack.

Event Details

Date: Wednesday, 8th of July 2026
Organizer: LEAP – AI & Data Consulting
Location: LEAP HQ, Østergade 36, 1. floor, 1100 Copenhagen

Agenda & Speakers
16:45 – Doors open

17:15 – Event introduction and welcome by Anders Boje Hertz (Co-Founder at LEAP)

17:30–19:30 – 💬 Speaker Sessions

dbt Core on Microsoft Fabric: What 6 Months in Production Looks Like
Speaker: Boris Kamber Garcevic (Business Intelligence Lead at DUOS)
DUOS runs dbt Core on Microsoft Fabric in production, 75-90 end users, 400+ models, 15 source systems, 5 business domains. Six months in, this talk shares what that actually looks like.
Trust, by design: an AI-ready platform with dbt
Speaker: Gaia-Emilie Wiendel Madsen (Senior Data & Platform Engineer at Novo Holdings)
Data teams still solve the same core problem: trust. At Novo Holdings, we built Atlas, a code-defined platform where governance does real work and transparency is surfaced where users work. This session explores the architecture and dbt patterns behind Atlas, and why metadata, lineage, and transparency are essential for AI-ready platforms.
dbt Core v2-dot-woah!
Speaker: Grace Goheen (Product Manager at dbt Labs)
dbt Labs just released the alpha of dbt Core v2: still open source, now rebuilt for what’s next. Powered by the same Rust engine behind dbt Fusion, v2 brings faster parsing, high-performance parquet artifacts, a single adapter layer, and more. Come learn more about what’s new and learn how to upgrade your project.

19:30 – 🍕 Let's eat. Enjoy pizzas and great conversations

20:30 – Wrap-up

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
For the best Meetup experience, join the #local-denmark channel in dbt Slack: https://slack.getdbt.com/

events.raw_text:

Wednesday, Mar 11 · 5:30 PM to 8:30 PM CET

Veo Technologies

Rovsingsgade 68, 2100 København · Denmark

Copenhagen dbt Meetup vol. 9

Hosted by Marie-Christin H.

Copenhagen dbt Meetup
4.7
67 reviews
Details

Ninth dbt Meetup in Copenhagen

The next Meetup is just around the corner! This time, we’re trying a new format: instead of three shorter presentations, we’ll feature two sessions — each with more time for deeper insights and discussion. The overarching theme for the evening is AI agents in analytics engineering and how they’re reshaping development with dbt.

Event Details

Date: Wednesday, 11th of March 2026
Organizer: Intellishore
Venue Host: Veo, Rovsingsgade 68, 2100 Copenhagen

Agenda & Speakers
17:30 – Kick off the evening by networking over drinks and snacks

17:45 – Event introduction and welcome

18:00–19:30 – 💬 Speaker Sessions

Building a dbt Data Vault That Scales With AI
Speaker: Frederik Juhl Pedersen (Senior Analytics Engineer at Veo)
Most dbt projects slow down as they grow — naming drifts, reviews pile up, onboarding drags. At Veo, we rebuilt our platform as a 5-layer Data Vault with AutomateDV, strict naming, and deterministic patterns. The payoff: AI agents generate production-ready models from specs, and swapping an ingestion tool only affects one layer. This session shares the key architecture decisions, tradeoffs, and how standardization turned AI into a force multiplier.

Agentic development with dbt Fusion and Databricks
Speaker: Nina Anderson (Solution Engineer at dbt Labs)
In this talk, we’ll explore agentic development with dbt Fusion, the dbt MCP server, and Databricks, showing how the technologies combine to build, test, and reason over analytics workflows autonomously and safely.

19:30 – 🍕 Let's eat. Enjoy pizzas and great conversations

20:30 – Wrap-up

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
For the best Meetup experience, join the #local-denmark channel in dbt Slack: https://slack.getdbt.com/

Lunar

Bredgade 43 · Copenhagen

Copenhagen dbt Meetup vol. 8

Hosted by Marie-Christin H.

Copenhagen dbt Meetup
4.7
67 reviews
Details

Eighth dbt Meetup in Copenhagen 🧡

We’re looking forward to welcoming you to the next dbt community evening with inspiring talks, great discussions and good company with other data enthusiasts!

Event Details

Date: Wednesday, 26th of November 2025
Organizer: Intellishore
Venue Host: Lunar, Bredgade 43, 1260 Copenhagen

Agenda & Speakers
17:30 – Kick off the evening by networking over drinks and snacks

17:45 – Event introduction and welcome

Hosted by Marie-Christin Henkelmann, Manager at Intellishore
and Imran Noon, Senior Analytics Engineer at Lunar

18:00–19:30 – 💬 Speaker Sessions

Refactoring dbt the Hard Way: Lessons Learned While Rebuilding Our Project Structure
Speaker: Olga Surkova (Senior Data Analyst at Keepit)
Refactoring a large dbt project isn’t just about renaming models - it’s about understanding what went wrong and redesigning for long-term scalability. In this talk, learn how the team at Keepit approached a full-scale dbt refactor, the challenges they faced, and what they learned along the way. You’ll see real examples of DAG restructuring, Jinja pattern improvements, and validation checks that kept everything running smoothly.

Building a Scalable Data Mesh with dbt and Snowflake at Velux
Speaker: Thomas Schrum Nicolet (Platform Engineer at Velux)
Learn how Velux is building a scalable data mesh from the ground up using dbt to design and model data products in Snowflake. This session explores how dbt serves as the single source of truth, and how the team uses custom and enhanced macros to simplify data engineering workflows and accelerate data delivery.

Building Resilient Data Pipelines for Embedded Analytics
Speaker: Martha Scheffler (Data Engineer at Qarma)
Discover how Qarma built a resilient data platform architecture using blue-green deployment strategies powered by Snowflake zero-copy clones and dbt macros. This talk also dives into orchestrating multiple dbt projects with Kestra - from serial execution for ingestion pipelines to parallel processing for scalable analytics delivery.

19:30 – 🍕 Let's eat. Enjoy pizzas and great conversations

20:30 – Wrap-up and final remarks

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/

Details

Seventh dbt Meetup in Copenhagen
Mark your calendar: Thursday, 12th of June 2025 🧡. We’re thrilled to invite you to an evening of inspiration, community, and all things dbt—featuring three insightful speakers from across the data world.

Event Details

Date: Thursday, 12th of June 2025
Organizer: Intellishore 🤝
Venue Host: Veo Technologies, Rovsingsgade 68, 2100 Copenhagen 🏠

Agenda & Speakers
17:30 - Kick off the evening by networking over drinks and snacks 🥤

17:45 - Event introduction and welcome

Hosted by Marie-Christin Henkelmann, Manager at Intellishore
and Carl Johan Rising, Director of Data & BI at Veo

18:00-20:00 - Speaker Sessions 💬

Metric Trees & AI – Spotify use case
Speaker: Johan Baltzar (Co-Founder & CEO at Steep)
Join Johan as he presents how to automatically analyze why your metrics are moving using Steep and dbt. He will share his experience of using metric trees from Spotify and why he is excited about this powerful AI use case.
Accelerating Data Products Delivery with dbt: LEO Pharma’s Success Story
Speaker: Nicola Barbieri (Principal Data Engineer at LEO Pharma) and Korina Kuhar (Data Engineering Lead at LEO Pharma)
Discover how LEO Pharma revolutionized data transformation with a smart, scalable design. Learn how Terraform and innovative dbt solutions empowered faster, more efficient delivery of data products.
For Builders, by Builders: The Latest Tools from dbt Labs
Speaker: Benoit Perigaud (Staff DX Advocate at dbt Labs)
Join Benoit to learn everything you need to know about Fusion, the new dbt engine, and what it changes for dbt users. The talk will go through what new capabilities are being brought to dbt as well as how to try the new engine in your own projects.

20:00 - 🍕 Let's eat - Enjoy pizzas and great conversations

20:45 - Wrap-up and final remarks 🙌🏼

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
For the best Meetup experience, join the #local-denmark channel in dbt Slack: https://slack.getdbt.com/

Pleo HQ

via. elevator directly from the courtyard, Sortedam Dossering 7, 4 sal, 2200 København · Copenhagen

Copenhagen dbt Meetup vol. 6
D

Hosted by dbt L. and Marie-Christin H.

dbt L. is a Super Organizer

Copenhagen dbt Meetup
4.7
67 reviews
Details

Sixth dbt Meetup in Copenhagen
Save the date: Wednesday, 26th of March 2025 🧡. We are excited to welcome three speakers who will share their knowledge and experiences with dbt.

Event Details

Date: Wednesday, 26th of March 2025
Organizer: Intellishore 🤝
Venue Host: Pleo – Sortedam Dossering 7, 4th floor, 2200 Copenhagen 🏠

Agenda & Speakers
17:30 - Kick off the evening by networking over drinks and snacks 🥤

17:45 - Event introduction and welcome

Hosted by Marie-Christin Henkelmann, Senior Consultant at Intellishore 💬

18:00-20:00 - Speaker Sessions 💬

Migrating dbt at Pleo: Handy tools & approaches
Speaker: Arın Tazeler (Pleo)
Pleo is undergoing through a lot of migrations including a new dbt repository, moving gradually from the old one. Arin will share how they leverage functions like column-level lingeage using custom solutions in dbt Core, and will dive into how Pleo approaches migrations with self-built tools.
Opinionated ESG Data Product Development with dbt
Speaker: Charles T. Gray (TDC Net)
Charles will present how to scale data governance using an opinionated, test-driven development strategy that emphasizes parallelized development, efficiency, and transparency. She will deep dive into test definitions for ESG reporting at TDC Net.
Building a strong semantic layer with dbt and Lightdash
Speaker: Kilian Tscherny (Skatteguiden)
Learn how Skatteguiden built a modern analytics stack with a strong semantic layer, to seamleassy integrate dbt with Lightdash for business insights. Kilian will present how they designed a scaleable, efficient data platform, why Lightdash has transformed their development flow, and how this approach has helped them stay agile as they reach 1 million users.

20:00 - 🍕 Food & Socializing - Continue conversations over food and drinks.

20:45 - Closing remarks and wrap-up 🙌🏼

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

Thu, Nov 21, 2024 · 8:00 PM to 9:00 PM CET

Online event

This event has passed

Community AMA with Erica Louie (dbt Labs)
Network event

99 attendees from 54 groups hosting

D

Hosted by dbt L.

Super Organizer

Copenhagen dbt Meetup
4.7
67 reviews
Details

REGISTER HERE 👉 https://www.getdbt.com/resources/webinars/community-slack-ama

🚀 Hey Community! Are you ready to dive deep, get insights, and satisfy your curiosity?

We’re hosting monthly live Ask Me Anything events! It's the perfect platform to quench your curiosity and get to know each other better. Don't miss out on this opportunity to connect and learn together.

What to Expect:

Discussing trends in the analytics space
Sharing key takeaways from the recent events
Hearing your questions and thoughts

Speaker: Erica Louie, Data @ dbt Labs
Host: Bolaji Oyejide, Digital Community Mgr @ dbt Labs
Topic: Careers in data, building a data team, the dbt Semantic Layer, and scaling your dbt project
When: November 21st @ 2pm EST
Where: Virtual and in #dbt-community-merge

Erica was the second employee at dbt Labs, then called Fishtown Analytics and she's seen dbt grow, a lot. In this AMA, Erica will share more on her career in data, her strong opinions on building a data team, how she manages her team, the dbt Semantic Layer, scaling your dbt project, and more on her Substack.

Register now to get the link to watch and join the #dbt-community-ama channel in Slack to participate live!

Read more
Speaker
Erica Louie, dbt Labs

Second hire at dbt Labs, former Head of Data, and now leading the product data team (and previously worked for BuzzFeed's data team), Erica "ric" Louie proudly hails from a liberal arts background (studied political science + italian) and grew her career in data at dbt Labs starting from the ground-up. She firmly believes that anyone can be a data practitioner with the right mentors, resources (sans trivial data jargon), and a good sense of humor. While rethinking the philosophy behind data systems, operations, and teams is one of her favorite topics, she enjoys thinking and writing about the human experience, and how it intersects with the work we choose to do and who we choose to do it with.

Details

Mark your calendars for the fifth dbt Meetup in Copenhagen! Thursday, the 10th of October 🧡

We have the speaker line-up ready for you, and we will have three exciting speakers!

Date: Thursday the 10th of October

🤝 Organizer: Intellishore

🏠 Venue Host: group.one - Kalvebod Brygge 24, 1560 København

Agenda & Speakers
17:30: Meet new people and catch up with old acquaintances over drinks and snacks 🥤

17:45: Event kick-off and Welcome - Hosted by Marie-Christin Henkelmann, Senior Consultant at Intellishore 💬

18:00-20:00 - Speaker sessions 💬

**Deep Dives on CI and Orchestration @ group.one by Emil Nilsson, Director of Data at group.one**
In this session, group.one will share their experiences with building a data team in a M&A-driven organisation, discussing key decisions and learnings along the way. Hear about specific aspects of their data stack, including CI processes and the orchestration of dbt jobs.

Schedule-less Data Architecture @ Ageras by August Hörlën, Senior Analytics Engineer at Ageras
Learn how Ageras transformed its data architecture by leveraging dynamic tables in Snowflake and dbt. This talk shows how they moved beyond traditional scheduling to create a responsive, schedule-less pipeline that ensures data is processed exactly when needed.

Late-stage transformations: Utilizing dbt Semantic Layer metrics by Erica Louie, Sr. Analytics Manager at dbt Labs & Andrew Escay, Lead Data Analyst at dbt Labs
Look at these beautiful dbt models! Why are we still experiencing the same friction with stakeholders?" This talk from experts at dbt Labs argues that we solved the first stage of building "Transformations" (via the 2024 State of Analytics Engineering report), and now we're now in the second stage: "The Philosophy of Transformations". And all roads lead to "metrics".

20:00: 🍕 Food & socializing - Continue the conversation over food and drinks.

20:45: Over and out 🙌🏼

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/

🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-denmark channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/)
----------------------------------
dbt is the standard in data transformation, used by over 40,000 organizations worldwide. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust. Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

Bredgade 43

Bredgade 43 · København

Copenhagen dbt Meetup vol. 4
J

Hosted by Jonatan Larsen E. and 2 others

dbt L. is a Super Organizer

Copenhagen dbt Meetup
4.7
67 reviews
Details

Get ready for the fourth dbt Meetup in Copenhagen! 🧡

We are blown away once again by the interest in our dbt Meetups, which, this time, were filled to max capacity in less than five days!! ❤️‍🔥

We can finally reveal the complete program, which you can see below, and sign up for the waiting list via the link. We are excited to have some very skilled and passionate people joining us for an exciting evening with our friends from Lunar at their office in Bredgade 🔥

FULLY BOOKED - All spaces taken for our dbt Meetup vol. 4.0!! 🚨 🚨 🚨You can still sign up for the waiting list if additional spaces open up.

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

Date: Wednesday the 15th of May

🤝Organizer: Intellishore

🏠Venue Host: Lunar - Bredgade 43, 1260 København K

Agenda & Speakers
17:30: Meet new people and catch up with old acquaintances over drinks and snacks 🥤

17:45: Event kick-off and Welcome - Hosted by Marie-Christin Henkelmann, Senior Consultant at Intellishore 💬

18:00-20:00 - Speaker sessions 💬

Working with dbt Core on the AWS stack by Mikael Hedegaard Thorup, Senior Analytics Engineer at Lunar
In this session, we will hear about Lunar's journey with dbt since 2019 and the present stack & architecture at Lunar. Furthermore, they will share the story of their recent user migration, where they modified ~300 dbt models in one, and show you some CLI/dbt hacks they used to ease the migration.

Implementing dbt and developing collaborative workflows, a math nerd's perspective by Charles T. Grey, Data Scientist at Mrs Wordsmith
In video game analytics, there is one data structure that defines the data architecture. Using dbt documentation, Mrs Wordsmith's Data Analytics team will share their experience of game event data pipeline optimization with examples from a data migration for Unity-created games.

Charting ZeroNorth's Voyage with Snowflake, Airflow, and DBT Core: A Closer Look at Our Infrastructure by Stephen O'Kennedy, Senior Data Engineer at ZeroNorth
Come along as ZeroNorth delves deeply into the process of deploying automated dbt pipelines at scale using open-source platforms. They will explore the tools utilized and the development lifecycle adhered to by their developers.

20:00: 🍕 Food & socializing - Continue the conversation over food and drinks.

20:45: Over and out 🙌🏼

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-denmark channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/)

dbt is a data transformation framework that lets analysts and engineers collaborate using their shared knowledge of SQL. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust.

Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

Rovsingsgade 68 · København

Copenhagen dbt Meetup vol. 3

Hosted by Anders Boje H. and 2 others

dbt L. is a Super Organizer

Copenhagen dbt Meetup
4.7
67 reviews
Details

We will start 2024 with a bang when we do our third dbt Meetup in Copenhagen!

The complete program is ready for our dbt Meetup vol. 3.0!! 🔥 🔥 🔥

We are proud and excited about the skillful and passionate people who agreed to spend their Wednesday evening with us and other devoted techies 🙏🏼

FULLY BOOKED - All spaces taken for our dbt Meetup vol. 3.0!! 🚨 🚨 🚨You can still sign up for the waiting list if additional spaces open up.

Date: Wednesday the 17th of January

🤝Organizer: Intellishore

🏠Venue Host: Veo Technologies - Rovsingsgade 68, 2100 København Ø

Agenda & Speakers
17:30: Meet new people and catch up with old acquaintances over drinks and snacks 🥤

17:45: Event kick-off and Welcome - Hosted by Anders Boje Hertz, Head of AI & Data platforms at Intellishore 💬

18:00-20:00 - Speaker sessions 💬

How to Use dbt and Reverse ETL to Power Your Data Activation by William Frisch Møller, Senior Data Engineer at Lundbeck
In this talk, Lundbeck will share how they use their modern data stack to support sales reps and increase sales with personalized suggestions in the Commercial organization. They will demonstrate how they use dbt to transform their data and Hightouch to sync it with their operational systems, delivering insights to the right people at the right time.

"Init" to win it by Magnus Enk, Head of BI at Veo Technologies & Bjarke Wheatley Enkelund, Analytics Engineer at Veo Technologies
Hear how Veo implemented dbt to address a pressing challenge - and how this choice slowly redefined their data stack. They will share how dbt helped them resolve one critical issue at a time and give examples of how they gradually applied engineering principles to their ways of working.

dbt mesh from beginner to advanced by Kshitij Aranke, Senior Software Engineer at dbt Labs
In this talk, the dbt team will cover what the new mesh paradigm unveiled at Coalesce is and steps you can take to make your project more "mesh"-ified:
Beginner – Model contracts, model versioning
Intermediate – Model governance with groups and access
Advanced – Cross-project references and setting up mesh with dbt-meshify

20:00: 🍕 Food & socializing - Continue the conversation over food and drinks.

20:45: Over and out 🙌🏼

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-denmark channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/)

Copenhagen dbt Meetup
4.7

Details

🤝Organizer: Intellishore

🏠Venue Host: Lederne - Vermlandsgade 65, 2300 København

Agenda & Speakers

17:30: Meet new people and catch up with old acquaintances over drinks and snacks

17:45: Event kick-off and Welcome (Hosted by Anders Boje Hertz, Head of AI & Data platforms at Intellishore & Marie-Christin Henkelmann, Senior Consultant at Intellishore)

18:00-20:00 - Speaker sessions
Petr Janda, Co-Founder & CEO at the Synq
- Building Reliable Data Platforms—With today's increasingly complex data systems, detecting errors is challenging. Inspired by lessons from dozens of dbt & data warehouse deployments, we will walk through how we've created a robust testing strategy to prevent issues in data platforms at Synq.

Mia Nørgaard Fugl, Senior Analytics Engineer at Pleo
- How we decided to build a new dbt data modeling repository and why we didn't want to fix our old one. A story about rolling out dbt at a company at scale and the do's and don'ts we learned along the way.

Migration to dbt and Snowflake: Lessons learned and technical walkthrough by Henrik Varmer, Head of Data & Analytics at Lederne
- We share valuable lessons from Lederne's experience migrating our data stack to Snowflake and dbt. You will get valuable insights from both the initial business and cost considerations prior to the migration, as well as a technical walkthrough of a real example of migrating complex data and business logic in dbt.

20:00: 🍕 Food & socializing - Continue the conversation over food and drinks.

20:45: Over and out

Details

🤝 Organizer: Intellishore
🏠 Venue: Møntergade 19, 1140 København

✨ Agenda & Speakers ✨

17:00: Meet new people and catch up with old acquaintances over drinks and snacks

17:15: Event kick-off and Welcome (Hosted by Anders Boje Hertz, Head of AI & Data platforms at Intellishore)

17:30: How Dagbladet Børsen went from complex to simple (Anders Boje Hertz at Head of AI & Data platforms at Intellishore)

18:00: LEGO Group - a centralized data architecture to decentralized data product ownership (Valeri Voev, Principal Data Engineer at the LEGO Group)
- The LEGO Group is on a journey to transition from a traditional model where data ingestion, transformation, quality assurance, etc., data value processes were done centrally to an enablement model with a decentralized data product ownership in the business domains

18:30: Multiple Solutions, Multiple Setups, Multiple Wins! - A Hands-on Look at Testing Techniques (Lisa-Marie Ebel, Analytics Engineering Lead at TooGoodToGo)
- Lisa-Marie will dive deep into the code and show their setup, focusing on GitHub Actions and the CI flow they have fine-tuned over time.

19:00: Input from the crowd on how these events will be the most fun ever! Continue the conversation over food and drinks.
